w:r.w:t: Complete AI Build Prompt & Design System
This document is your complete operating manual for building FORMA — a classy, conversion-focused SaaS Landing Page Builder — using an AI coding assistant (Cursor, Claude, ChatGPT, or similar).
Each phase in this document contains one or more prompts that you copy and paste directly into your AI. After each phase, you will see a red STOP block. That block means:
Do not ask the AI for anything else.
Review the output in your browser or code editor.
Only move to the next phase when you have confirmed it works and you are ready.

The backend phases are intentionally withheld until you explicitly command the AI to proceed. This ensures the frontend is solid, the design is pixel-perfect, and you are not wasting time building on an unstable foundation.

Every prompt in this document contains the exact language, design tokens, component names, and instructions the AI needs. You do not need to explain the design separately — it is embedded in each prompt.

FORMA is built around a single aesthetic principle: restrained luxury. The design must feel like a premium editorial product — warm cream surfaces, deep wine accents, refined serif typography, and motion that breathes rather than performs.

Do not allow the AI to use Inter, Roboto, or any system sans-serif font. Do not allow purple gradients, blue buttons, or card shadows that look like Material Design. If the AI deviates from the design system below, correct it immediately before continuing.
JetBrains Mono — Code blocks and labels
All animations must feel like a breath — not a performance. The following are the only animation styles permitted in FORMA:
Entrance Fade-Up — Elements enter from 18px below with opacity 0 → 1, duration 0.6s, ease: cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1), staggered by 80ms per child.
Underline Reveal — On hover over links and nav items, a 1px wine-colored underline grows from left to right using scaleX(0) → scaleX(1) with transform-origin: left, duration 0.3s ease-out.
Button Press — Buttons scale down to 0.97 on mousedown, spring back on release. Duration 150ms. No glow, no shadow pulse.
Panel Slide-In — The properties sidebar slides in from the right: translateX(100%) → translateX(0), duration 0.35s, ease: cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1).
Section Highlight Pulse — When a section is selected in the builder, its border pulses once: opacity 0 → 1 → 0.6 → 1, duration 0.5s. No looping.
Drag Ghost — While dragging a section, the dragged item renders at 70% opacity with a subtle wine border. The drop target shows a 2px wine line.
Skeleton Loader — Before content loads, show a shimmer animation: linear-gradient moving from left to right in wine-muted tones. No spinners.
No other animations are permitted. No bouncing, no rotating loaders, no particle effects, no confetti. If the AI adds any animation not listed above, instruct it to remove it.
Use this prompt exactly once, at the very beginning of the project. Do not modify it.
You are building a SaaS Landing Page Builder called FORMA.
Initialize the project with the following stack:
- Google Fonts: Cormorant Garamond, DM Sans, JetBrains Mono
Set up the global CSS variables in globals.css:
Set the default font to DM Sans.
Set heading font to Cormorant Garamond.
Create the following folder structure under /src:
Do not build any UI yet. Only set up the project.
When done, confirm the folder structure and installed packages.
◆ STOP — PHASE 0 COMPLETE — Review folder structure before continuing ◆
Do not proceed. Wait for a user command before continuing to the next phase.
This phase builds all five section components as static, styled React components using the FORMA design system. No interactivity yet — just perfect markup and visual fidelity.
In /src/types/index.ts, define the following TypeScript interfaces:
Also export a DEFAULT_PAGE constant with a single hero section:
subheadline: 'A refined, powerful landing page builder for serious products.',
Build the Hero section component at /src/components/sections/HeroSection.tsx
- Background: use props.bgColor, defaulting to var(--color-cream)
- No image backgrounds, no gradient overlays, no blobs or shapes
- Layout: full-width, centered, min-height 92vh, flex column, center aligned
- Above the headline: a small all-caps label in DM Sans, 11px,
- Headline: Cormorant Garamond Bold, 72px, color var(--color-charcoal),
- CTA Button: background var(--color-wine), text white,
- Below CTA: a subtle hairline divider in var(--color-divider),
- Label animates: opacity 0 → 1, y 12 → 0, delay 0s, duration 0.5s
Do not add any other UI. No navbar yet. Just the Hero.
- Background: var(--color-wine-muted) — the pale blush tint
- Section label above title: DM Sans 11px, letter-spacing 0.14em,
- Title: Cormorant Garamond Bold, 48px, color var(--color-charcoal),
· No card shadow, no rounded corners
· Icon: a simple geometric icon (use Lucide React),
· Feature title: DM Sans SemiBold, 15px, color var(--color-charcoal)
· Feature body: DM Sans, 14px, color var(--color-stone), line-height 1.65
1. Drag & Compose — 'Rearrange sections with precision. Every movement intentional.'
2. Live Editing — 'Click any element to edit it. Changes reflect in real time.'
3. Export Ready — 'Export clean HTML/CSS or React components with one click.'
- Cards animate in with fade-up, staggered 120ms each, on scroll using
Framer Motion's whileInView with once: true
Prompt 1.4 — Build Testimonials, Pricing, and Footer Sections
- Section label: 'WHAT THEY SAY', same style as Features label
· A thin top rule in var(--color-divider)
· Quote in Cormorant Garamond Italic, 20px, color var(--color-charcoal)
· Author name: DM Sans SemiBold 13px, letter-spacing 0.06em, all-caps
· Author role: DM Sans 13px, color var(--color-stone)
- Default 3 placeholder testimonials with realistic copy
· Starter: $29/mo — Build up to 5 pages, export HTML
· Pro: $79/mo — Unlimited pages, AI generator, live publish
- CTA buttons: outlined (border 1px white, transparent bg)
- Pro plan has a small badge: 'MOST POPULAR' in gold (var(--color-gold))
- Left: FORMA wordmark in Cormorant Garamond, white, 24px
- Right: 'Built with FORMA.' in DM Sans 13px
- A thin top divider in rgba(255,255,255,0.08)
- No social links, no extra columns — keep it minimal
◆ STOP — PHASE 1 COMPLETE — Review all 5 section components before continuing ◆
Do not proceed. Wait for a user command before continuing to the next phase.
This phase converts the static sections into a live, data-driven builder. The page is no longer hardcoded — it renders from a JSON structure stored in Zustand.
The store must manage the following state:
The store must expose the following actions:
addSection(type: SectionType) — adds a new section at the end
with a generated ID and default props for that type
deleteSection(id: string) — removes section from array
updateSectionProp(id, key, value) — updates a single prop
on the specified section without mutating others
undo() — moves historyIndex back by 1 and restores that snapshot
redo() — moves historyIndex forward by 1 and restores that snapshot
Every mutation except selectSection must push the current state
to history before applying the change (max history depth: 30).
Use immer middleware for clean state mutations.
The Canvas renders all sections from the Zustand store in order.
For each section in the store's sections array:
- Render the matching component (Hero, Features, etc.) with its props
- Wrap it in a SectionBlock (built next prompt)
- Pass the section's id and props down
Use a switch statement or lookup map to resolve section type → component.
- Takes the full available height of the editor area
- No padding of its own — let sections handle their own padding
When no sections exist, show a centered empty state:
- Icon: a plus symbol in var(--color-wine), 32px
- Text: 'Your canvas is empty.' in Cormorant Garamond Italic, 22px, stone
- Subtext: 'Use the toolbar to add your first section.' in DM Sans 14px, stone
SectionBlock wraps each section in the Canvas and handles selection.
- On click: call selectSection(id) from the store
· Show a 2px wine border around the entire section block
· Show a small floating label at the top-left corner of the block:
background var(--color-wine), white DM Sans 11px text
· On mount of the border, animate it:
· On hover: show a thin 1px var(--color-divider) dashed border
· Show a small floating menu at the top-right:
· These only appear on hover (opacity transition 200ms)
Position: relative (to contain the floating label and menu).
Do not add any other controls yet.
◆ STOP — PHASE 2 COMPLETE — Confirm dynamic rendering and section selection work ◆
Do not proceed. Wait for a user command before continuing to the next phase.
This phase assembles the full builder layout — the left toolbar, center canvas, and right sidebar that appears when a section is selected.
Build the main editor layout at /src/app/editor/page.tsx
Layout: full viewport height, no scroll on the root
- No labels, only icons with Tooltips on hover
- A thin right border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.06)
- This is where the Canvas component renders
- A subtle grain texture overlay: use a repeating SVG noise pattern
at 4% opacity — gives depth without competing with content
RIGHT COLUMN — Sidebar (width: 320px, visible only when selectedId !== null)
- Slides in from the right using Framer Motion:
TOP BAR — above all three columns (height: 52px)
- Left: FORMA wordmark (Cormorant Garamond Bold 20px, wine)
- Center: page title input (editable inline, DM Sans 14px, charcoal)
- Right: Undo, Redo icon buttons + 'Preview' outlined button +
- All top bar buttons: DM Sans 13px, no rounded corners,
Do not implement the Sidebar content yet. Just render its shell with
a placeholder message: 'Select a section to edit its properties.'
The Toolbar contains buttons to add sections to the canvas.
Each button adds a new section via addSection() from the store.
- On hover: icon becomes white, background becomes rgba(255,255,255,0.06)
- Active state (if this section type exists on canvas): icon color var(--color-gold)
- Tooltip on hover (right side): DM Sans 12px, background var(--color-charcoal),
white text, padding 6px 10px, no rounded corners
Add a divider between the section buttons and two utility buttons at the bottom:
All transitions on the toolbar buttons: 180ms ease
The Sidebar renders when a section is selected.
It reads the selected section from the Zustand store
and renders controls based on the section's props.
- Section type name: DM Sans SemiBold 13px, all-caps, charcoal,
- Close button (X icon) at the right — calls selectSection(null)
Each prop renders as a labeled control group:
- Label: DM Sans 11px, all-caps, letter-spacing 0.1em, stone
Control types based on prop name/value:
→ Textarea (auto-resize), DM Sans 14px, border 1px solid var(--color-divider),
focus border var(--color-wine), no rounded corners, no box-shadow,
→ A color swatch button that opens a native color picker,
showing current color as a 24x24 square (no rounded corners)
next to a small hex code in DM Sans Mono 12px
→ A minimal toggle switch: track in var(--color-divider),
active track in var(--color-wine), thumb white 18px circle,
Every change must call updateSectionProp(id, key, value) from the store.
All updates are live — no submit button.
At the bottom of the sidebar: a 'Delete Section' text link
(DM Sans 13px, color var(--color-wine), no underline on default,
underline on hover) — calls deleteSection(id) and closes the sidebar.
◆ STOP — PHASE 3 COMPLETE — Confirm sidebar slides in, controls update the canvas live ◆
Do not proceed. Wait for a user command before continuing to the next phase.
This phase adds dnd-kit for section reordering and a responsive preview mode that shows how the page looks on mobile vs desktop.
Implement drag-and-drop section reordering using dnd-kit.
Wrap the Canvas with DndContext and SortableContext from dnd-kit.
Each SectionBlock becomes a useSortable item.
- A small 6-dot grid icon (GripVertical from Lucide) at the left edge
of each SectionBlock, visible only on hover
- The dragged section renders at 70% opacity
- Apply a 2px wine border to the dragged section's ghost
- The drop target shows a 3px horizontal line in var(--color-wine)
above the target position — not a full border, just a line
- Call reorderSections() with the new array order from arrayMove
Sensor: PointerSensor with a 5px activation distance
(to avoid accidental drags when clicking to select)
Do not add keyboard sensor — keep it pointer only.
Add a responsive preview mode to the editor.
In the top bar, add two icon buttons after the Undo/Redo group:
- Monitor icon (desktop view, active by default)
- The canvas area constrains to a centered container, max-width 390px
- A subtle phone frame appears around it: rounded-none top/bottom,
- All section components must already be responsive (enforce this now
if any section is not — fix it)
- The canvas background outside the phone frame: var(--color-wine-muted)
Switching between views must be animated:
- The canvas container width transitions with CSS transition: width 0.35s ease
Store the current view mode (desktop | mobile) in a local React state,
◆ STOP — PHASE 4 COMPLETE — Confirm drag/drop reorders, preview toggle works ◆
Do not proceed. Wait for a user command before continuing to the next phase.
Before any backend work, build the templates system and the dashboard landing page. These complete the front-end experience.
1. blank.ts — empty sections array, title: 'Blank Page'
Each template exports a TemplateDefinition object with:
The gallery is a modal (overlay) triggered by a 'Templates' button
in the top bar of the editor.
- Overlay: rgba(42, 34, 40, 0.7) — the charcoal at 70% opacity
- Modal panel: white, no rounded corners, max-width 780px,
- Header: 'Choose a Template' in Cormorant Garamond Bold 32px,
· A preview thumbnail area (140px height, background wine-muted,
centered section type count label in stone)
· Template name: DM Sans SemiBold 14px, charcoal
· 'Use Template' button: wine, full width, no rounded corners
- On 'Use Template': load that template's sections into the store
This is the page a user sees before entering the editor.
It is NOT a marketing page — it is a minimal dashboard.
- Top navigation: FORMA wordmark (left), 'New Project' button (right)
- 'New Project' button: wine background, white DM Sans 13px, no rounded corners,
- Page title: 'Your Projects' — Cormorant Garamond Bold 34px, charcoal
- Subtitle: 'Start from a template or open an existing project.'
Project cards in a 3-column responsive grid:
- For now, show 3 placeholder project cards (static, no backend yet):
· A thin top border: 3px solid var(--color-wine)
· Project name: Cormorant Garamond Bold 20px, charcoal
· Last edited placeholder: DM Sans 12px, stone
· On hover: background transitions to white,
· Reveal real cards after 800ms with a staggered fade-up
- One line, DM Sans 12px, stone, centered:
'FORMA — Built for builders who care about craft.'
◆ STOP — PHASE 5 COMPLETE — Front-end is now complete. Do not add backend yet. ◆
Do not proceed. Wait for a user command before continuing to the next phase.
This phase is still front-end only. It implements the export feature as a client-side JSON → HTML conversion with no server required.
Build an HTML export utility at /src/utils/exportHTML.ts
This function converts the sections JSON to a complete, standalone HTML file.
- Embed all CSS inline in a <style> tag in the <head>
- Use the FORMA design tokens as CSS variables
- Load Cormorant Garamond and DM Sans from Google Fonts
- Be self-contained — no external JS, no framework dependencies
For each section type, generate semantic, accessible HTML:
- features → <section class='forma-features'> with <ul> of feature items
- pricing → <section class='forma-pricing'> with two <div class='tier'> cards
Inject each section's props as the actual content.
The generated HTML should look pixel-close to the builder canvas.
All colors, fonts, and spacing must match the design system exactly.
Return the full HTML string including <!DOCTYPE html> and <html> wrapper.
Then wire up the 'Export' button in the top bar:
- Show a brief toast notification: 'Page exported successfully'
The toast appears at the bottom center, auto-dismisses after 3s,
background var(--color-charcoal), white text, no rounded corners
Do not build a React export. HTML only.
◆ STOP — PHASE 6 COMPLETE — Test export in browser. Confirm the downloaded HTML is clean. ◆
Do not proceed. Wait for a user command before continuing to the next phase.
The following phases introduce the backend. Do not share these prompts with your AI until you have explicitly decided to begin backend work. Each phase must be commanded individually.
When you are ready for backend: tell your AI 'Begin Phase 7 — Firebase Setup'.
Phase 7 — Firebase Setup & Project Save (Unlock When Ready)
Say to your AI: 'Begin Phase 7 — Firebase Setup'
Set up Firebase for project persistence.
Initialize Firebase at /src/lib/firebase.ts using environment variables:
Build these three utility functions in /src/lib/db.ts:
- saveProject(userId, title, sections): Promise<string> — returns doc ID
Wire up the 'Save' button in the top bar (add it now if not there):
- On save: write to Firestore, show toast 'Project saved'
- Auto-save every 60 seconds if the page has unsaved changes
- Track unsaved state with a small dot indicator next to the page title
Say to your AI: 'Begin Phase 8 — Authentication'
Auth method: Google Sign-In only (no email/password for now)
- Wraps the dashboard and editor routes
- Minimal, centered layout on cream background
· White background, charcoal border 1px, DM Sans 14px
Store user state in a context provider at /src/context/AuthContext.tsx
- Load only the authenticated user's projects from Firestore
- Show a 'Sign Out' button in the top nav
- Show the user's display name next to the sign-out button
Phase 9 — AI Section Generator (Unlock When Ready)
Say to your AI: 'Begin Phase 9 — AI Generator'
Add an AI-powered section generator to FORMA.
Add an 'AI Generate' button to the Toolbar (Sparkles icon from Lucide).
On click: open a modal prompt input:
focus border var(--color-wine), no box-shadow, no rounded corners
Placeholder: 'e.g. A landing page for a luxury skincare brand in Lagos.'
- 'Generate' button: wine, DM Sans 13px, no rounded corners
The route calls the Anthropic Claude API (claude-sonnet-4-20250514):
'You are a landing page architect for FORMA. Given a product description,
return a JSON array of sections. Each section must have: id (use uuid),
The copy must be specific to the described product, not generic.
Return only valid JSON. No markdown, no explanation.'
User prompt: the text the user typed
- Load the sections into the Zustand store (replacing current)
- Close the modal, show toast: 'Page generated. Review and refine.'
While generating: show a skeleton shimmer on the canvas
On error: show toast: 'Generation failed. Try again.' in wine background.
Store the API key as ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in .env.local — never expose it.
Phase 10 — Publish to Live URL (Unlock When Ready)
Say to your AI: 'Begin Phase 10 — Publish'
When a user clicks 'Publish' in the top bar:
1. Call exportToHTML(sections) to generate the HTML string
2. Upload it to Firebase Storage at:
3. Set the file's Cache-Control to: public, max-age=3600
4. Get the download URL from Firebase Storage
5. Save the URL to the project's Firestore document as 'publishedUrl'
- The live URL as a clickable link
- A 'Copy URL' button (copies to clipboard, shows 'Copied!' for 2s)
- An 'Open Page' button (opens in new tab)
In the top bar, if a project has a publishedUrl:
- Show a small green dot next to the 'Publish' button
◆ STOP — ALL PHASES COMPLETE — Review the full product before any polish pass ◆
Do not proceed. Wait for a user command before continuing to the next phase.
Use these prompts after all phases are done to audit and elevate the product.
Audit the entire FORMA UI for design consistency.
1. Every button must use border-radius: 2px — no pill shapes, no full radius
2. No font other than Cormorant Garamond, DM Sans, or JetBrains Mono
should appear anywhere in the UI
3. No purple, blue, or green should appear anywhere — report any violations
4. All focus states must use outline: 2px solid var(--color-wine)
with outline-offset: 2px — replace any default browser focus rings
5. Every interactive element must have a cursor: pointer
6. The Sidebar must not show a scrollbar unless content overflows —
use a styled scrollbar: 4px, wine color, rounded thumb
7. Verify all text is readable at WCAG AA contrast ratio
8. Remove any animation not listed in the FORMA permitted animation list
Report all violations found, fix them, and confirm when done.
Audit FORMA for performance and accessibility.
- All icon-only buttons must have aria-label attributes
- All form inputs must have associated <label> elements
- The canvas sections must have role='region' and aria-label
- Drag handles must have aria-roledescription='drag handle'
- Color contrast must meet WCAG AA for all text
- Wrap all section components in React.memo
- The Zustand store selectors must use shallow equality where arrays are selected
- Heavy components (TemplateGallery modal, Sidebar) should be lazily loaded
- Verify no layout thrashing occurs when the Sidebar slides in
Fix all issues found. Do not change any visual design while fixing.
Make FORMA fully responsive for tablets (768px) and large screens (1440px+).
- The Sidebar should become a bottom sheet instead of a side panel
- The Toolbar should move to the top as a horizontal icon bar
- The Canvas should take full width
- Current 3-column layout (Toolbar / Canvas / Sidebar) stays as designed
- Top nav collapses: FORMA wordmark stays, 'New Project' becomes a + button
- Hero: font scales to 44px on tablet, 32px on mobile
- Features: 2 columns on tablet, 1 column on mobile
- Pricing: stacked vertically on tablet and mobile
- Testimonials: 2 columns on tablet, 1 on mobile
Do not use any UI framework for responsive layout.
Use only Tailwind responsive prefixes (sm:, md:, lg:).
Build with intention. Ship with confidence.
